perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement Cremer shall supply Ultragenyx with the Product free from defect and meeting the product specification attached to this Agreement as Annex A (the "Product Specifications").
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement Cremer shall supply Ultragenyx with the goods conforming to general industry standards.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the Product must meet the precise specifications outlined in Annex A, providing a clear, measurable standard. The changed text replaces this with 'goods conforming to general industry standards,' which is vague and undefined. This introduces uncertainty because general industry standards are subjective and may conflict with the Product Specifications in Annex A, making enforcement challenging. This change introduces a contradiction because Annex A provides a very specific set of quality control parameters, and "general industry standards" can be interpreted more loosely. The term 'Product' is effectively redefined, which means that the standards of "Product" can vary according to the interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement Cremer shall supply Ultragenyx with the goods conforming to general industry standards.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the clear 'Product Specifications' of Annex A with the vague 'general industry standards.' This creates a direct contradiction because Annex A provides specific quality control parameters, while 'general industry standards' are open to interpretation and may not align. Since the changed text and the contradicted text are the same, it is implied that the contract now has two conflicting definitions or standards of quality for the product being supplied, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The prices payable by Ultragenyx to Cremer for the Product (the "Price") shall be agreed [***] every contract year; provided, that the Price may not increase more than the [***] for such period or [***]%, whichever is higher. At the date of signing the Parties agree on a Price of €[***] per kilogram for the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: The charges payable by Ultragenyx to Cremer for the Product (the "Price") shall be unilaterally determined by Cremer every contract year. At the date of signing the Parties agree on a Price of €[***] per kilogram for the Product.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates that the 'Price' must be 'agreed' upon annually, suggesting a mutual negotiation process. The modified text asserts that the charges will be 'unilaterally determined by Cremer,' contradicting the prior agreement of mutual consent. The contradiction creates uncertainty since the initial agreement suggested the price was not Cremer's decision alone, which opposes the altered modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The charges payable by Ultragenyx to Cremer for the Product (the "Price") shall be unilaterally determined by Cremer every contract year. At the date of signing the Parties agree on a Price of €[***] per kilogram for the Product.
perturbation.justification: While the explanation highlights a potential conflict in the decision-making process for price determination ('agreed' vs. 'unilaterally determined'), the changed text and contradicted text are identical. The fact that the price is unilaterally determined does not, in and of itself, contradict the clause stating that 'At the date of signing the Parties agree on a Price of €[***] per kilogram for the Product.' Both clauses could be in the same section with the same overall intended meaning. Therefore, based purely on the provided snippets, the change doesn't create a sufficiently strong and direct in-text contradiction. The modification of the original text alone doesn't trigger a contradiction with the identical Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Cremer represents and warrants that all quantities of Product delivered under the Agreement were manufactured in accordance with GMP. The Product shall be free from defects if it is within the specifications according to Annex A.
perturbation.changed_text: Cremer states that all quantities of the Substance provided under this Agreement were produced in alignment with standard manufacturing practices. The Substance shall be free from defects based on Cremer's assessment.
perturbation.explanation: In the original agreement, Cremer 'represents and warrants' compliance with GMP and adherence to specifications in Annex A. The modified version weakens these assurances by replacing 'represents and warrants' with 'states' and replacing 'Product' with 'Substance', as well as saying that the Product shall be free from defects based on Cremer's assessment. This introduces ambiguity about the level of assurance and the governing standards. The introduction of 'Substance' creates confusion as to whether 'Substance' and 'Product' are the same and contradicts the original precision. Further, the change of standard from specifications outlined in Annex A to 'Cremer's assessment' is not measurable and entirely subjective, therefore it also makes it challenging.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ultragenyx will perform final release of the Product. Ultragenyx may rely on the documentation provided by Cremer and Ultragenyx will not need to independently test the Product unless Ultragenyx determines such independent testing is necessary. In the event that the Product fails to conform to the Product Specifications, and/or GMP, Ultragenyx may reject the Product by giving written notice to Cremer within [***] days after receipt of the Product and all documentation (except such [***] day period will not apply for any latent defect). Within [***] days following receipt of the rejected and returned Product from Ultragenyx, Cremer will, at Ultragenyx's choice, replace such quantity of Product with Product conforming to the Product Specifications, and GMP or refund Ultragenyx the Price paid for such Product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces significant contradictions. The original text specified adherence to 'GMP' and 'Product Specifications according to Annex A', providing clear, objective standards for product quality. The changed text replaces these with 'standard manufacturing practices' and 'Cremer's assessment,' introducing ambiguity and subjectivity. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Ultragenyx can reject the product if it fails to conform to the 'Product Specifications' and 'GMP'. The changed text makes it unclear what standards Ultragenyx can rely on for rejecting the product, creating a contradiction. Also the text mentions the replacement of 'Product' by 'Substance' in the Changed Text, which introduces Misaligned Terminology that leads to in-text contradictions and creates confusion about the obligations.
